failure_chain: Recon detected Next.js framework with 42 items in DOM
Strategy chose http + dom_parse (should have chosen browser)
Executor fetched HTML (877K chars) but extractor found 0 products
Retried 3 times with same strategy, same result
Validator marked as FAILED after max retries
repair_recommendations: Install Playwright: playwright install chromium
SPA detection should trigger when framework=nextjs AND items_extracted=0
Use Nike's __NEXT_DATA__ JSON for extraction (product wall extractor exists)
Consider using nike.com/gb/ or nike.com/w/ specific product listing URLs

available_extractors.note: Extractor exists and works with fixture data (test_nike_extracts_product_wall_json_list passes)

failure_chain: DOM parser found repeated elements in navigation bar
Navigation items have title, price, and image fields
Parser treated nav items as products with 100% confidence
Actual product grid was not reached
repair_recommendations: Use GTM data-gtm attribute extractor (available and tested)
Target specific product listing URLs (e.g., /womens/tops) instead of homepage
Improve DOM parser to prefer .product-tile over navigation elements
Add depth penalty for elements inside <nav> or header regions

failure_chain: Found first number match: '13' followed by more digits
Regex matched '1326' as a single number (dashes stripped)

affected_items.title: Square Neck Mini Shift Dress with Linen

repair_recommendations: Use parse_price_range() before _number_or_none() for price fields
Store both min_price and max_price when range detected
Update ecommerce extractors to handle range patterns
parse_price_range() already works correctly: £13 - £26 → {min: 13, max: 26}

diagnosis.hang_reason: live_probe=True triggers browser rendering, Playwright not installed

repair_recommendations: Add timeout to inspect_access live_probe (e.g., 30s)
Fall back to static analysis when browser unavailable
Check Playwright availability before attempting browser probe

possible_causes: run_profile_longrun may not use merged profile selectors
Crawl runner may use its own DOM analysis instead of patched selectors
Test limit too small (5) combined with partial run

repair_recommendations: Verify run_profile_longrun uses profile selectors
Add logging to show which selectors the crawl runner actually uses
Test with higher test_limit to rule out small-sample issues
The managed loop has follow_pagination action but it requires pagination_urls in extra_context
reanalyze_site should detect <a> pagination links and pass them
Consider adding pagination detection to the base crawl graph
